bead.title: enforce configurable lock hold-time cap with fail-fast release and violation surfacing
bead.description:
PROBLEM
Even after locks are scoped correctly, a bug or hang could still cause a worker to hold .git/index.lock or .ddx/.git-tracker.lock indefinitely, regressing to the cascading-timeout failure mode observed 2026-05-17. DDx needs a backstop that detects over-long holds and surfaces them loudly.

ROOT CAUSE
No current code path enforces a maximum hold time on these locks. Executing agent should extend the instrumented-lock helper introduced in [[lock-lifetime-metric]] so the same wrapper that emits metrics can also enforce a cap.

PROPOSED FIX
Add configurable caps (default 10s for .git/index.lock, 30s for .ddx/.git-tracker.lock) read from .ddx.yml or env (DDX_LOCK_CAP_INDEX_MS, DDX_LOCK_CAP_TRACKER_MS). When a holder exceeds the cap: log an error event with stack trace, force-release the lock, and write a violation record to the worker's execution evidence directory (.ddx/executions/<run-id>/lock-violation.json). The worker continues but the violation is visible to the post-execution reviewer.

NON-SCOPE
- Adding caps to locks other than the two named above
- Killing the offending worker process (release-only behavior here)
- Auto-retry of the operation that exceeded the cap
- Metric emission (handled by [[lock-lifetime-metric]]).
bead.acceptance:
1. TestLockCap_DefaultIndexLockCap10s asserts the default cap for .git/index.lock is 10s and is configurable via env.
2. TestLockCap_ExceedingCapForceReleases asserts that holding past the cap triggers a forced release and the underlying file is removed.
3. TestLockCap_ViolationWrittenToEvidence asserts a lock-violation.json appears under the worker's .ddx/executions/<run-id>/ with lock_name, cap_ms, actual_hold_ms, holder_pid, stack.
4. TestLockCap_ViolationLoggedAsError asserts an error-severity event is emitted via the metric helper.
5. cd cli && go test ./internal/lockmetrics/... ./cmd/... green.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
